title: Data & Statistics
objective: Students will explore data & statistics concepts including Sorting into categories.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Sorting into categories!
Classify objects into given categories, count the number in each category, and sort the categories by count
Let's practice together! Sort a set of shapes by colour and count how many in each group
Can you sort a pile of objects — like buttons, coins, or toy animals — into groups and count how many are in each group?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Sorting into categories. Sort a set of shapes by colour and count how many in each group Sort objects by size (big/small) and state how many in each category
Let's explore What Is a Half?! Have you ever wondered about this?
Recognise, find, and name a half as one of two equal parts of an object, shape, or quantity
Let me show you something cool. Fold a shape into two equal parts and identify each as 'a half'
If you is helping share a sandwich equally between two people, can they cut it so both pieces are the same size — and tell you each piece is called a half?
Great job! Today we learned about What Is a Half?. Fold a shape into two equal parts and identify each as 'a half' Find half of 8 objects by sharing into 2 equal groups
Have you ever thought about Finding halves and quarters (age 5+)? Let's find out together!
Recognise, find, and name a quarter as one of four equal parts of an object, shape, or quantity
Time to try this out: Fold a shape into four equal parts and identify each as 'a quarter'
If you fold a square piece of paper into four equal parts, can you tell you that each part is a quarter — and point to just one quarter?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Finding halves and quarters (age 5+): Fold a shape into four equal parts and identify each as 'a quarter' Find a quarter of 12 objects by sharing into 4 equal groups
